long_stay: Upper secondary education within an exchange programme
Full-time, year-long preparatory language courses organized by universities for international students
Institutional traineeships regulated by EU frameworks
qualifying_courses.notes: Standalone language courses or part-time programs do not qualify for a study visa.

types.name: Long-stay Study Visa (Type D)
types.notes: Allows multiple entries and is an essential requirement for a Bulgarian residence permit.

booking_methods.description: Booking can be done online via the embassy's website, or via email/telephone.
In certain regions, appointments may be booked directly via outsourced visa handling agencies.

steps.title: University Admission
steps.description: Apply and get accepted into an accredited Bulgarian higher education institution for a full-time program.

steps.title: Ministry Approval
steps.description: The university submits your documents to the Bulgarian Ministry of Education and Science (MES), which issues an official Certificate of Admission/Eligibility.

steps.title: Book Embassy Appointment
steps.description: Book an appointment at the Bulgarian Embassy or Consulate in your country of citizenship or permanent residence.
steps.action_required: Book appointment after receiving the MES certificate.

steps.title: Submit Documents and Pay Fee
steps.description: Submit all required physical documents (translated into Bulgarian and legalized) and pay the D-visa application fee.
steps.action_required: Attend appointment, submit documents, pay fee, provide fingerprints if required.

steps.title: Visa Processing
steps.description: Allow time for the Ministry of Foreign Affairs to approve and issue the D-visa.

steps.title: Apply for Residence Permit
steps.description: After arriving in Bulgaria with your Type D visa, you must apply for a long-term residence permit.
steps.action_required: Apply at the Migration Directorate after arrival.

required_documents.name: Valid Passport
required_documents.details: Valid for at least 12 months beyond your intended stay, with at least two blank pages.

required_documents.name: Completed and Signed Visa Application Form
Letter of Acceptance from a Bulgarian University
Ministry of Education Certificate of Eligibility/Admission
required_documents.details: Original certificate issued by the Bulgarian Ministry of Education and Science (MES).

required_documents.name: Proof of Paid Tuition Fees
required_documents.details: Bank transfer document proving payment for at least the first semester or full year.

required_documents.name: Bank Statements Showing Financial Means
required_documents.details: Certified personal bank statements from an account in the student's name.

required_documents.name: Travel Health Insurance
required_documents.details: Valid across the EU/Bulgaria, covering repatriation, urgent medical care, and emergency hospital treatment, with minimum coverage of €30,000 (or 60,000 BGN).

required_documents.name: Proof of Accommodation
required_documents.details: A notarized lease agreement or an official accommodation certificate from a university student dormitory.

required_documents.name: Police Clearance Report (Criminal Record Certificate)
required_documents.details: Issued from your home country or country of residence, legalized or apostilled.
required_documents.notes: Must be original, legalized, and translated.

required_documents.name: Medical Certificate
required_documents.details: A general medical check-up clearance issued by a doctor in your home country.

acceptable_proof: Certified personal bank statements from a personal account in the student's name
Notarized sponsorship declaration accompanied by sponsor's bank records

residence_permit.notes: The 5-day deadline is specifically for address registration upon arrival. The Long Term Residence Permit is issued for one year. A temporary residence certificate is issued, and the card within three weeks. Application fee is 11 BGN, plus administrative fees (e.g., 40 BGN for approval, 45 BGN for regular ID card service).

work_while_studying.notes: No separate work permit is required for students.

required_documents: Fresh Certificate of Enrollment from university (proving successful advancement)
permit_renewal.notes: The renewal process is similar to the initial application. The residence permit must be renewed every academic year.

requirements: Register with the Employment Agency to use brokerage employment services
Secure a job offer matching academic qualifications
Employer must prove no suitable Bulgarian or EU candidate is available for the job (labor market test)
post_study_work.notes: Students can apply to extend their residence permit for up to 9 months to seek employment or start a business. Conversion to a standard Single Work and Residence Permit requires the employer to apply to the Employment Agency.

official_sources.name: Bulgarian Ministry of Foreign Affairs
official_sources.description: For visa types, specific diplomatic guidelines, and consular locations.

official_sources.name: EU Immigration Portal – Student in Bulgaria
National Migration Directorate
official_sources.description: For physical residence permit protocols inside the country.

official_sources.name: Bulgarian Ministry of Education and Science (MES)
official_sources.description: For educational institutional criteria and validation rules.

application_forms.name: Residence Permit Application Form
application_forms.authority: National Migration Directorate, Regional Migration Directorates, local police station Immigration Department, regional university international offices

common_mistakes.mistake: Applying for a language course expecting a student visa
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Only full-time university programs or preparatory courses organized by universities qualify for a student visa, not standalone language courses.

common_mistakes.mistake: Submitting documents not translated into Bulgarian
common_mistakes.consequence: Documents will be rejected, leading to delays or visa refusal.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: All documents must be officially translated into Bulgarian and legalized/apostilled before submission.

common_mistakes.mistake: Applying too late for the visa
common_mistakes.consequence: May miss your course start date.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Apply at least 6–8 weeks before your course start date; visa processing can take up to 3 months.
Show at least 1,500 BGN (~€770) per month in your personal bank account.

common_mistakes.mistake: Missing the address registration deadline upon arrival
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Register your address with the Migration Directorate within 5 days of arriving in Bulgaria.
Obtain the original, legalized, and translated criminal record certificate from your home country before leaving.
Ensure your policy covers at least €30,000 and is valid across the entire EU.
Documents like criminal record certificates and diplomas must be properly apostilled or legalized in your home country.

common_mistakes.mistake: Insufficient funds history in bank statements
common_mistakes.consequence: Raises red flags for consular officers and may lead to visa rejection.
common_mistakes.how_to_avoid: Ensure the money in your bank account appears stable; avoid drastic, unverified deposits right before submitting the visa request.
